page: Microsoft Azure D16plds_v5 is an ARM64-based virtual machine belonging to the DPLDSv5 server family. It is powered by an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 3.0 GHz, delivering 16 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes a bundled 614 GB local disk and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

Benchmark evaluations position the D16plds_v5 in the average performance tier for most multi-core and memory operations. The instance achieved a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 28883.33 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 9656.0. Memory bandwidth is measured at 119.88 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 68.21 GB/sec for write operations. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1092.0 and text processing performance categorized as weak. Additionally, while prompt processing for a small 135M LLM model reached an average speed of 412.43 tokens/sec, text generation speed was poor at 30.85 tokens/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture provides a dense physical core layout without hyperthreading, which can reduce resource contention for parallel workloads. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this server suitable for compute-centric tasks that do not require massive memory footprints. Appropriate workloads include static web serving, where it demonstrated a throughput of 17.29 GB/sec, software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, and database operations such as Redis, which achieved 20.65 million SET operations per second.
description: Microsoft Azure D16plds_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine from the DPLDSv5 family, featuring an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor with 16 dedicated physical cores running at 3.0 GHz. It is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per core) and a bundled 614 GB local disk. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance offers a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it a compute-focused option. Performance benchmarks place the server in the average tier for multi-core tasks, such as software compilation and database operations, though single-core and text processing speeds represent a performance tradeoff. With a static web serving throughput of 17.29 GB/sec and 20.65 million Redis SET operations per second, this server is well-suited for web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and scale-out workloads that benefit from dedicated physical ARM cores without hyperthreading.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16plds_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores, 32 GB of RAM, and a 614 GB local disk, optimized for compute-centric workloads and web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16plds_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine with 16 dedicated Ampere cores, 32 GB RAM, and 614 GB local storage for compute workloads.
tagline: An ARM64-powered compute instance featuring sixteen dedicated physical cores and bundled local storage for efficient parallel workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 16-core Ampere Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor at 3.0 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM and a 614 GB local storage disk
Delivers 17.29 GB/sec throughput for static web serving
Achieves 20.65 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Best suited for multi-threaded decompression, web serving, and software compilation
Tradeoffs include lower single-core performance and text processing speeds
